title: AI Security News, Incidents & OSINT Tools
description: Sources, dashboards, and tools for tracking AI security incidents, vendor disclosures, regulatory actions, and the rapidly-evolving threat landscape.

tools.name: AI Incident Database (Partnership on AI)
tools.blurb: Open-source database of AI incidents — categorized, sourced, dated.
tools.our_take: The reference incident database. Submit reports; cite entries; cross-reference with our coverage.

tools.name: OECD AI Incidents Monitor
tools.blurb: Government-tier incident tracking — slower but authoritative.
tools.our_take: Use as a credibility signal. If OECD has an entry, the incident has been independently verified by multiple sources.

tools.name: MITRE ATLAS Case Studies
tools.blurb: Adversarial attack case studies on real ML systems — formatted to ATT&CK-style technique mapping.
tools.our_take: Case studies are smaller in number but deeper in technical detail than other databases.

tools.name: tldrsec
tools.blurb: Weekly security newsletter — covers AI security alongside traditional security.
tools.our_take: Required reading. Curation quality is high; filters noise effectively.

tools.name: Risky.Biz
tools.blurb: Long-running infosec podcast/newsletter; AI security is a recurring beat.
tools.our_take: The podcast is one of the few infosec shows that gets AI right. The newsletter is solid.

tools.name: Embedded.ai
tools.blurb: AI policy and risk newsletter from Helen Toner.
tools.our_take: Less day-to-day operational; more policy and capability landscape.

tools.name: OpenAI Trust Portal
tools.blurb: OpenAI's security disclosures and SOC 2 documentation.
tools.our_take: Subscribe to RSS. Vendor-tier disclosures appear here before press coverage.

tools.name: Anthropic Trust Center
tools.blurb: Anthropic's compliance and security disclosure portal.
tools.our_take: Same playbook as OpenAI. Subscribe to updates.

tools.name: Hugging Face Security
tools.blurb: HF's vulnerability disclosure procedures and security advisories.
tools.our_take: Most ML supply-chain CVEs land here first.

tools.name: EU AI Act compliance tracker
tools.blurb: Independent tracker of EU AI Act implementation milestones, secondary legislation, and provider obligations.
tools.our_take: Better than the official EUR-Lex source for following implementation pace.

tools.name: Stanford HAI AI Index
tools.blurb: Annual report on AI capability, deployment, and policy landscape.
tools.our_take: The single best annual snapshot of where AI is. Cite it; reference it.

tools.name: GPTZero
tools.our_take: Detection accuracy is unreliable enough that we don't recommend using it for high-stakes decisions. Inform readers; don't gate on it.

tools.name: DetectGPT
tools.blurb: Research-grade AI text detector based on probability curvature.
tools.our_take: Better methodology than commercial tools but suffers the same fundamental problem: detection is brittle to paraphrase and editing.
